title: Calibration and Quantification
text: LIBS calibration converts measured peak area (intensity ratio) elemental concentration using set matrix-matched standards.

title: Calibration and Quantification
code: cal_ds <- ls_example_data("calibration") cal_ds conc <- cal_ds$sample_info$concentration unique(conc) #> [1]     0   100   250   500  1000  2500  5000  7500 10000

title: Calibration and Quantification
code: cal <- ls_calibrate(cal_ds, element = "Ca", line_nm = 393.37,                     concentrations = conc, method = "univariate",                     verbose = FALSE) cal

title: Calibration and Quantification
code: ls_plot_calibration(cal)

title: Calibration and Quantification
code: ls_plot_residuals(cal)

title: Calibration and Quantification
text: semi-quantitative estimates without standards, use Saha-Boltzmann method:
code: spec <- ls_simulate_spectrum(   elements = c(Ca = 10000, Fe = 500, Na = 2000),   n_channels = 2048, seed = 7 ) spec <- ls_baseline(spec, method = "snip", iterations = 60) ls_saha_boltzmann(   spec, elements = c("Ca", "Fe"),   lines_nm = list(     Ca = c(422.673, 445.478, 487.813),     Fe = c(371.994, 404.581, 438.354)   ),   verbose = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   element temperature_k concentration_rel electron_density n_lines r_squared #>   <chr>           <dbl>             <dbl>            <dbl>   <int>     <dbl> #> 1 Ca           -113215.             0.595             1e17       3     0.993 #> 2 Fe             53153.             0.405             1e17       3     0.144

title: Getting Started with libscanR
text: libscanR provides vendor-agnostic pipeline Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy (LIBS) data, including import, preprocessing, peak detection, calibration, quantification, chemometrics, spatial mapping, visualization. ships curated NIST emission line database example datasets every feature runnable without real instrument data.

title: Getting Started with libscanR
text: libscanR defines three S3 classes: libs_spectrum — single spectrum (possibly multi-shot). libs_dataset — collection spectra sharing wavelength axis. libs_calibration — fitted calibration model.

title: Getting Started with libscanR
code: spec <- ls_simulate_spectrum(   elements = c(Ca = 5000, Na = 1000, Fe = 200),   n_channels = 1024,   seed = 1 ) spec #> <libs_spectrum> #> • Range: 200-900 nm (1024 channels) #> • Shots: 10 #> • Sample: "simulated" (synthetic) #> • Baseline corrected: FALSE

title: Getting Started with libscanR
code: ls_plot_spectrum(spec, show_elements = c("Ca", "Na"))

title: Getting Started with libscanR
code: spec_proc <- spec |>   ls_baseline(method = "snip", iterations = 40) |>   ls_smooth(method = "moving_avg", window = 5) |>   ls_normalize(method = "total") ls_plot_spectrum(spec_proc)

title: Preprocessing Workflow
text: Four methods supported: snip, als, rolling_ball, linear, polynomial. SNIP ALS workhorses LIBS continuum removal.
code: spec <- ls_simulate_spectrum(   elements = c(Ca = 5000, Na = 1000, K = 800, Fe = 200),   n_channels = 1024, seed = 1 ) snip <- ls_baseline(spec, method = "snip", iterations = 60) als  <- ls_baseline(spec, method = "als", iterations = 10, lambda = 1e5)  df <- rbind(   data.frame(w = spec$wavelength,              y = libscanR:::.mean_intensity(spec),              method = "raw"),   data.frame(w = spec$wavelength,              y = libscanR:::.mean_intensity(snip),              method = "SNIP"),   data.frame(w = spec$wavelength,              y = libscanR:::.mean_intensity(als),              method = "ALS") ) ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(w, y, colour = method)) +   ggplot2::geom_line() + theme_libs() +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Wavelength (nm)", y = "Intensity (a.u.)")

title: Preprocessing Workflow
text: Five options:
code: norm_total <- ls_normalize(snip, method = "total") cat("sum after total normalization:",     round(sum(norm_total$intensity[1, ]), 2), "\n") #> sum after total normalization: 1  norm_is <- ls_normalize(snip, method = "internal_std", ref_wavelength = 589)

title: Preprocessing Workflow
code: sm <- ls_smooth(snip, method = "moving_avg", window = 7) ls_plot_region(sm, 380, 410)

what: Analysis and Visualization of Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy Data
title: Analysis and Visualization of Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy Data
text: Vendor-agnostic analysis visualization Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy (LIBS) data R, focus biomedical tissue applications.

title: Analysis and Visualization of Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy Data
text: Import: Generic CSV/TSV/TXT, SciAps Z-series, Applied Spectra J200/Aurora, auto-detection. Preprocessing: SNIP/ALS baseline correction, five normalization modes, Savitzky-Golay / Gaussian / moving-average / median smoothing, shot averaging outlier removal, wavelength cropping, gate delay optimization. Peak analysis: SNR+prominence peak detection; identification curated NIST emission-line database (23 biomedically relevant elements). Calibration: Univariate, internal-standard, PLS, calibration-free LIBS (Saha-Boltzmann). LOD / LOQ 3σ / 10σ criteria. Chemometrics: PCA, PLS-DA, k-means / hierarchical clustering, SVM / Random-Forest classifiers. Tissue analysis: Rule-based tissue classification using elemental ratios; discriminating-line analysis FDR correction. Spatial mapping: Single-element multi-element 2-D maps raster-scan datasets. Visualization: Publication-ready ggplot2 plots; custom theme_libs() wavelength color scale. Shiny app: Six-tab interactive explorer (ls_run_app()). Reproducible examples: ls_example_data() provides synthetic tissue, calibration, spatial datasets — instrument data required.

code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("r-heller/libscanR")

title: Analysis and Visualization of Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy Data
code: library(libscanR)  # Generate a synthetic spectrum spec <- ls_simulate_spectrum(   elements = c(Ca = 5000, Na = 1000, Fe = 200),   seed = 1 )  # Preprocess: baseline, smooth, normalize spec_proc <- spec |>   ls_baseline(method = "snip") |>   ls_smooth(method = "moving_avg", window = 5) |>   ls_normalize(method = "total")  # Detect and identify peaks peaks <- ls_find_peaks(spec_proc) id <- ls_identify_peaks(peaks, elements = c("Ca", "Na", "Fe"))  # Plot with element annotation ls_plot_spectrum(spec_proc, show_elements = c("Ca", "Na"))  # Launch the interactive app ls_run_app(data = ls_example_data("tissue"))

title: Baseline Correction — ls_baseline
text: Removes continuum/baseline drift LIBS spectra. Supported methods: "snip" (Statistics-sensitive Non-linear Iterative Peak-clipping, Morhac/Ryan), "als" (Asymmetric Least Squares smoothing, Eilers & Boelens), "rolling_ball" (morphological), "linear" (two-endpoint interpolation), "polynomial" (low-order polynomial fit baseline-regions).

title: Baseline Correction — ls_baseline
code: ls_baseline(   x,   method = "snip",   iterations = 100,   order = 3,   lambda = 1e+05,   p = 0.01,   radius = 50,   ... )

title: Baseline Correction — ls_baseline
text: x ls_spectrum() ls_dataset() object. method Character. One "snip", "als", "rolling_ball", "linear", "polynomial". Default "snip". iterations Integer. Iterations iterative methods (SNIP, ALS). Default 100. order Integer. Polynomial order "polynomial". Default 3. lambda Numeric. Smoothness parameter ALS. Default 1e5. p Numeric. Asymmetry parameter ALS (0 < p < 1). Default 0.01. radius Integer. Rolling-ball radius channels. Default 50. ... Reserved future use.

title: Baseline Correction — ls_baseline
text: class input baseline-corrected intensities; estimated baseline stored baseline element inspection.

code: ls_calibrate(   dataset,   element,   line_nm,   concentrations,   method = "univariate",   internal_std_nm = NULL,   window_nm = 1,   pls_window_nm = NULL,   n_components = 5,   unit = "ppm",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Build Calibration Curve — ls_calibrate
text: dataset ls_dataset() containing standard spectra. Spectra order must correspond concentrations. element Character. Target element symbol. line_nm Numeric. Emission line wavelength (nm). concentrations Numeric vector known concentrations, one per spectrum dataset. method Character. "univariate" (default), "internal_std", "pls", "cf_libs". internal_std_nm Numeric. Internal standard line wavelength, required method = "internal_std". window_nm Numeric. Peak integration window. Default 1. pls_window_nm Numeric vector length 2. Spectral window PLS. Default c(line_nm - 5, line_nm + 5). n_components Integer. Number PLS components. Default 5. unit Character. Concentration unit. Default "ppm". verbose Logical. Default TRUE.

title: Create a LIBS Calibration Model — ls_calibration

title: Create a LIBS Calibration Model — ls_calibration
text: element Character. Element symbol (e.g. "Ca", "Fe"). wavelength_nm Numeric. Emission line wavelength used. concentrations Numeric vector. Known concentrations standards. intensities Numeric vector. Measured peak intensities areas (ratios internal_std). length concentrations. model Fitted model object (e.g. lm, mvr). method Character. One "univariate", "internal_std", "pls", "cf_libs". Default "univariate". lod Numeric. Limit detection (3-sigma). Default NULL. loq Numeric. Limit quantification (10-sigma). Default NULL. r_squared Numeric. Coefficient determination. Default NULL. unit Character. Concentration unit, e.g. "ppm", "wt_pct", "mg_kg". Default "ppm".

title: Create a LIBS Calibration Model — ls_calibration
text: libs_calibration S3 object.

title: Create a LIBS Calibration Model — ls_calibration
code: conc <- c(100, 500, 1000, 2500, 5000) ints <- conc * 0.12 + stats::rnorm(5, 0, 5) m <- stats::lm(conc ~ ints) cal <- ls_calibration("Ca", 393.37, conc, ints, model = m,                       r_squared = summary(m)$r.squared) print(cal) #> <libs_calibration> #> • Element: "Ca" #> • Line: 393.37 nm #> • Method: "univariate" #> • Unit: "ppm" #> • Standards: 5 #> • R-squared: 0.9995 #> • LOD: (not set) #> • LOQ: (not set)

title: Create a LIBS Dataset — ls_dataset
code: specs <- lapply(1:3, function(i) {   ls_simulate_spectrum(elements = c(Ca = 1000 * i, Na = 500), seed = i) }) specs <- Map(function(s, id) {   s$metadata$sample_id <- paste0("s", id)   s }, specs, seq_along(specs)) ds <- ls_dataset(specs) print(ds) #> <libs_dataset> #> • Spectra: 3 #> • Channels: 2048 #> • Range: 200-900 nm #> • Groups: 1 (material)

title: NIST Emission Line Database — ls_element_db
text: Returns curated subset NIST Atomic Spectra Database containing analytically useful emission lines LIBS analysis, focus biomedically relevant elements. Data compiled NIST Atomic Spectra Database (https://physics.nist.gov/asd); values reference use users requiring high-precision spectroscopy consult NIST ASD directly.

title: NIST Emission Line Database — ls_element_db

title: Tissue Classification via LIBS — ls_tissue_classify
text: Classifies tissue types based LIBS spectral signatures. Implements three methods: "ratio" — rule-based using canonical elemental ratios (Ca/Na, Fe/Na, Zn/Na, K/Na, C/Ca) derived tissue biochemistry. training data required. "plsda" — fits PLS-DA model supplied labelled reference dataset predicts class x. "svm" — fits SVM reference predicts class.

Default TRUE.

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_read_spectrum(): Read individual LIBS spectra CSV/TSV/TXT. ls_read_dir(): Batch-import spectra directory. ls_read_csv(): Convenience wrapper CSV files. ls_read_sciaps(), ls_read_aurora(): Vendor-specific parsers. ls_read_auto(): Auto-detect format dispatch correct reader. ls_write_csv(), ls_export_spectra(): Export processed data.

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: S3 classes: libs_spectrum, libs_dataset, libs_calibration. Print, summary, subset, plot, length, dim, combine methods. Helper predicates: is_libs_spectrum(), is_libs_dataset(), is_libs_calibration().

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_baseline(): SNIP, ALS, rolling ball, linear, polynomial. ls_normalize(): Total, max, SNV, internal standard, area. ls_smooth(): Savitzky-Golay, moving average, Gaussian, median. ls_average_shots(): Shot averaging outlier removal. ls_crop(): Spectral range subsetting. ls_gate_optimize(): Optimize acquisition gate delay.

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_find_peaks(): Peak detection SNR, prominence, distance filters. ls_identify_peaks(): Match detected peaks NIST database. ls_peak_area(): Integrate peak area baseline subtraction. ls_element_db(): Curated NIST emission lines 23 elements.

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_calibrate(): Univariate, internal standard, PLS calibration. ls_saha_boltzmann(): Calibration-free LIBS (Boltzmann plot). ls_quantify(): Apply calibration unknowns LOD/LOQ flags. ls_lod(), ls_loq(): Figures merit.

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_pca(): Principal component analysis. ls_plsda(): PLS-DA classification cross-validation. ls_cluster(): K-means hierarchical clustering. ls_train_classifier(): SVM Random Forest training. ls_classify(): Predict class unknown spectra.

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_build_map(): Build elemental distribution maps. ls_map_elements(): Multi-element map construction.

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_tissue_classify(): Ratio-based model-based tissue classification. ls_tissue_discriminate(): Identify discriminating emission lines.

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_plot_spectrum(), ls_plot_overlay(), ls_plot_region(): Spectrum plots. ls_plot_calibration(), ls_plot_residuals(): Calibration diagnostics. ls_plot_map(), ls_plot_map_panel(), ls_plot_element_map(): Elemental maps. ls_plot_pca(), ls_plot_loadings(), ls_plot_scree(): PCA plots. ls_plot_plsda(): PLS-DA diagnostic plots. theme_libs(), scale_color_wavelength(): ggplot2 helpers.

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_run_app(): Interactive spectral explorer six tabs (Import, Preprocess, Peaks, Calibration, Chemometrics, Export).

title: libscanR 0.1.0
text: ls_simulate_spectrum(): Realistic synthetic LIBS spectra. ls_example_data(): Tissue, calibration, spatial scenarios.
